engineIntelligence.h2: Suzuki Swift Plus Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the K12M 1.2L petrol engine fitted to the Suzuki Swift Plus sedan in the UK grey import market, including documented failure causes, variant production years 2015-2017, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Suzuki Swift Plus K12M engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Idle control valve failure - causes rough idle and stalling after 60k miles on high-mileage imports.
Timing chain tensioner wear - cold-start rattle develops from 70k miles if oil changes delayed.
VVT solenoid fault P0011 - triggers check engine light and reduced power on urban-driven units.

cards.altText: Suzuki Swift Plus K12M-VVT engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
productionHistory: Swift Plus ZXi sedan with VVT (2015-2017)
knownFailures: VVT oil control solenoid clogging - causes cam timing errors and poor fuel economy on short-journey imports.
Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear after 80k miles leads to blue smoke on acceleration.

cards.altText: Suzuki Swift Plus K12M-AGS engine - petrol 1.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: AGS transmission calibration fault - juddering during gear changes requires ECU sync after engine replacement.
Idle control motor failure - causes high idle or stalling when AGS system engages on cold start.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,650-£2,400 (supply only) Estimated from comparable K12M units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Suzuki Swift Plus Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Suzuki Swift Plus K12M 1.2L petrol engine is generally reliable but known for idle control valve wear, timing chain tensioner noise and VVT solenoid faults in UK grey import use. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Idle Control Valve Failure - Why Swift Plus Engines Stall at Idle
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Swift Plus VXi, ZXi (2015-2017, K12M)
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup on the idle air control valve restricts airflow, causing unstable idle speed and stalling when coming to a stop.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, clean and recalibrate idle control valve
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may recur within 12 months on high-mileage imports

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new idle control valve with ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable solution if timing chain and VVT system are healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded IACV, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for engines over 70k miles or with multiple fault codes

problems.recommendation: On a Swift Plus worth £3,000-£5,000 as a grey import, IACV cleaning is worth trying first if the engine is otherwise healthy. If the car has over 70k miles or shows additional fault codes, a rebuilt K12M engine with upgraded components offers better long-term value and UK MoT compliance assurance.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K12M engine prices for your Suzuki Swift Plus

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Noise - Cold-Start Rattle on K12M Engines
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Swift Plus VXi, ZXi (2015-2017, K12M)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes metallic rattling on cold start until oil pressure builds.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioner and guide rails
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if chain shows minimal stretch; requires specialist timing tools

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, chain, guides, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for engines over 75k miles or with existing chain stretch

problems.recommendation: If the rattle disappears within 2 seconds of cold start and the engine has under 70k miles, tensioner replacement may suffice. For Swift Plus imports over 75k miles or with persistent noise, a rebuilt K12M engine with upgraded timing components prevents catastrophic failure and protects your UK registration investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K12M timing chain replacement Suzuki Swift Plus

problems.h4: VVT Solenoid Fault P0011 - Cam Timing Error on Swift Plus
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Swift Plus VXi (O), ZXi, ZXi Plus (2016-2017, K12M-VVT)
problems.rootCause: Oil sludge or debris clogs the VVT oil control solenoid, preventing proper cam phasing and triggering P0011 fault code with reduced power.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or replace VVT solenoid, reset ECU, test drive
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; may recur if oil change intervals exceeded

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded VVT system, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage imports or recurring P0011 after solenoid replacement

problems.recommendation: For a Swift Plus grey import used for UK urban delivery, solenoid replacement is cost-effective if the engine has clean service history. If oil changes were missed or the car has over 75k miles, a rebuilt K12M-VVT engine with upgraded VVT components offers peace of mind and MoT compliance.
problems.cta: Compare K12M-VVT engine prices for Suzuki Swift Plus P0011 fix

problems.h4: Oil Consumption Issue - Piston Ring Wear on High-Mileage K12M
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Swift Plus all variants (2015-2017, K12M)
problems.rootCause: Piston rings wear or stick due to extended oil change intervals, allowing oil to enter combustion chamber and burn, causing blue smoke and low compression.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace piston rings, valve seals, gaskets; machine head if needed
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block and crank are undamaged; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded rings, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for engines with blue smoke, low compression or over 80k miles

problems.recommendation: If your Swift Plus shows blue smoke on acceleration or fails a compression test, a top-end rebuild may work if caught early. For grey imports over 80k miles or with confirmed low compression, a rebuilt K12M engine with upgraded piston rings offers the most reliable long-term solution for UK road use.
problems.cta: Get quotes for K12M engine rebuild Suzuki Swift Plus oil consumption

problems.h4: AGS Transmission Calibration Fault - Judder on Auto Swift Plus
problems.affectedModels: Suzuki Swift Plus VXi AGS, ZXi AGS (2016-2017, K12M-AGS)
problems.rootCause: AGS actuator or ECU calibration drift causes harsh or delayed gear changes, often triggered after battery disconnect or engine replacement without proper sync.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Connect diagnostic tool, perform AGS adaptation reset and road test
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if no mechanical wear; requires AGS-capable diagnostic equipment

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace AGS actuator motor, recalibrate system
repairOptions.longevity: Needed if actuator shows mechanical wear or error codes persist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with AGS sync verification, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage AGS imports or combined engine/transmission faults

problems.recommendation: For AGS-equipped Swift Plus imports showing judder but no mechanical noise, recalibration is the logical first step. If the car has over 65k miles or shows multiple AGS fault codes, a rebuilt K12M-AGS engine with pre-verified AGS calibration ensures smooth urban driving and UK MoT compliance.
problems.cta: Compare K12M-AGS engine prices for Suzuki Swift Plus AGS fix

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Suzuki Swift Plus
finalCta.paragraph: The K12M engine in your Swift Plus is generally robust but has known failure points that can escalate if ignored. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Suzuki Swift Plus specialists who understand grey import compliance. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty with nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and keep your Swift Plus on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Suzuki Swift Plus Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Suzuki Swift Plus and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.question: How much does a Suzuki Swift Plus K12M engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Suzuki Swift Plus K12M engine typically costs £1,395-£2,200 supply-only in the UK, with AGS-calibrated units ranging £1,650-£2,400. Prices vary by trim, mileage and supplier location.
keyPoints: Base K12M rebuilt units start around £1,395 supply-only
VVT and AGS variants command £100-£300 premium due to calibration complexity
Supply & fit service adds £450-£850 depending on workshop location
All EngineMarket quotes include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Suzuki Swift Plus K12M engine prices UK

items.question: What engine code does my Suzuki Swift Plus have - K12M, K12M-VVT or K12M-AGS?
items.answer: All Suzuki Swift Plus sedans 2015-2017 use the K12M 1.2L petrol family; VVT variants have enhanced cam timing, AGS variants include automated manual calibration.
keyPoints: Base VXi/ZXi manual: K12M code
VXi (O)/ZXi Plus with VVT: K12M-VVT code
AGS automatic variants: K12M-AGS code
Enter registration above for instant DVLA-verified engine code lookup
items.cta: Verify your Suzuki Swift Plus engine code instantly

items.question: Can I use a used Suzuki Swift Plus engine from India for UK registration?
items.answer: Yes, but verify the engine code matches your chassis, obtain export de-registration documents, and ensure the unit passes UK MoT emissions testing before DVLA registration.
keyPoints: Confirm K12M code compatibility with your Swift Plus chassis number
Request Indian export certificate and de-registration paperwork
Verify compression and emissions compliance before UK shipment
EngineMarket specialists assist with DVLA documentation requirements

items.question: Do I need ECU recoding for a Suzuki Swift Plus engine swap UK import?
items.answer: Most Suzuki Swift Plus K12M swaps require only basic ECU reset; VVT and AGS variants need specialist calibration verification to ensure smooth operation post-replacement.
keyPoints: Base K12M engines typically need throttle adaptation reset only
K12M-VVT units require VVT solenoid learning procedure
K12M-AGS engines need AGS actuator calibration and road test
EngineMarket specialists include necessary calibration in supply & fit service
items.cta: Find Suzuki Swift Plus ECU calibration specialists UK

items.question: What oil should I use after Suzuki Swift Plus engine replacement UK import?
items.answer: Use 5W-30 semi-synthetic oil meeting API SN specification for rebuilt Suzuki Swift Plus K12M engines, with change intervals of 6,000 miles for urban grey import use.
keyPoints: 5W-30 viscosity optimal for UK climate and stop-start urban driving
API SN specification ensures compatibility with K12M components
6,000-mile change interval recommended for high-mileage imports
Oil specification documented in warranty terms for compliance verification
